title: Detect registry and implementation drift with reviewed closeout plans
goal: Find merged implementations, runtime evidence and lifecycle states that diverge, then produce reviewed closeout plans without blind verification.

acceptance.assertion: Doctor identifies merged implementation with open task, verified task still queued, closed PR without decision, runtime proof without closeout and queue/state lane mismatch.
A merged PR is evidence to inspect, not sufficient by itself; acceptance and current tests are rechecked.
Any lifecycle or queue mutation requires a reviewed, hash-bound plan with rollback or refusal semantics.
The detector cannot verify tasks, merge PRs or dispatch work.

metadata.source: Bureau total audit and Truth Model v2 design
